issues.title: chunk pipeline: couple docs to GitHub state to prevent backlog/run-log drift
issues.body_raw:
**Complexity:** M
**Benefit:** High

## Background

Chunk-pipeline state lives in two hand-edited markdown files that drift from GitHub reality:

- `docs/CHUNK_BACKLOG.md` — forward-looking plan (chunks, phases, prereqs).
- `AGENTIC_RUN_LOG.md` — backward-looking ledger (one row per merged chunk).

GitHub itself (issues + PRs + labels) is the actual source of truth for "open / closed / merged", but neither file reflects GitHub state automatically.

### Drift observed on 2026-05-05

`docs/CHUNK_BACKLOG.md` listed #1, #14 as open and chunks 1–3 + 5 as todo. Reality: all merged days earlier (#81, #82, #84, #88, #89). Required a hand-edit (commit `58963df`) to reconcile.

### Additional findings while auditing

- `scripts/agentic/run_log.py` exposes `append_chunk_row()`, but **no chunks-CLI subcommand calls it**. All rows in `AGENTIC_RUN_LOG.md` were typed by hand.
- `AGENTIC_RUN_LOG.md` is at the repo root; spec §8 step 5 and handbook §11.2 specify `docs/AGENTIC_RUN_LOG.md`. Path drift, not a code bug.
- Off-pipeline issue closures (#1, #83, #85, #86, #90) never propagated into the backlog because nothing checks GitHub.

## Proposal

Make GitHub the source of truth; the markdown files become rebuilt artifacts. Four parts (full design in `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-05-chunk-docs-github-coupling-design.md`):

1. **YAML-as-source for the backlog.** Move chunk metadata (name, issues, phase, prereqs, notes) into `docs/chunks-backlog.yaml`. Status is **never** stored — GitHub already knows.
2. **`scripts/agentic/chunks render-backlog`** — joins the YAML with `gh issue list/view` + `gh pr list` and rewrites `docs/CHUNK_BACKLOG.md` with a populated Status column. `--check` mode for CI.
3. **Auto-write `AGENTIC_RUN_LOG.md`.** Wire `chunks complete <name>` to call the existing `run_log.append_chunk_row()`. Move the file to `docs/AGENTIC_RUN_LOG.md` (where the spec already locates it).
4. **`scripts/agentic/chunks reconcile`** — diffs the markdown vs GitHub state and exits non-zero on drift. Suitable for manual hygiene checks and CI gates.

## Operator workflow after this lands

- Issue closes (any path) → next `chunks render-backlog` run produces a fresh `CHUNK_BACKLOG.md`. No hand-edit.
- Chunk merges → `chunks complete <name> --pr-url <url>` writes the run-log row automatically.
- CI runs `chunks reconcile` → blocks PRs that introduce drift.

## Files to touch

- `docs/chunks-backlog.yaml` (new) — translated content of current `docs/CHUNK_BACKLOG.md`.
- `scripts/agentic/chunks` — new subcommands `render-backlog`, `reconcile`; wire `complete` to call `run_log.append_chunk_row`.
- `scripts/agentic/templates/chunks-backlog.md.j2` (new) — render template.
- `scripts/agentic/run_log.py` — already has `append_chunk_row`; no code change expected.
- `AGENTIC_RUN_LOG.md` → `docs/AGENTIC_RUN_LOG.md` (git mv).
- `tests/agentic/test_render_backlog.py` (new), `tests/agentic/test_reconcile.py` (new).
- `CLAUDE.md` — session-start protocol note about `chunks reconcile`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `docs/chunks-backlog.yaml` exists with the current backlog content translated.
- [ ] `chunks render-backlog` produces output byte-identical (modulo a timestamp line) to a hand-curated reference for the current state.
- [ ] `chunks render-backlog --check` exits 1 when stale, 0 when fresh.
- [ ] `chunks complete <name> --pr-url <url>` appends a row to `docs/AGENTIC_RUN_LOG.md` using `run_log.append_chunk_row`.
- [ ] `AGENTIC_RUN_LOG.md` (root) moved to `docs/AGENTIC_RUN_LOG.md` via `git mv`.
- [ ] `chunks reconcile` reports drift correctly and exits accordingly.
- [ ] Unit tests under `tests/agentic/` cover renderer output, `--check` exit codes, and a synthesized drift case.

## Out of scope

- GitHub Projects integration (revisit later if YAML approach proves brittle).
- Pushing per-chunk `status.json` back to GitHub labels/comments.
- Reconciling the three overlapping planning docs (handbook / playbook / spec).

## Notes

- Open question logged in the spec: keep `docs/CHUNK_BACKLOG.md` as a committed generated artifact (noisy diffs but readable on GitHub web) vs git-ignore it (clean history but no web view). Tentative: keep it, gate via `--check` in CI.
- `gh` call budget: use one bulk `gh issue list` rather than per-issue `gh issue view` to avoid rate-limit waste.
- This is itself a chunk-pipeline correctness ticket and follows the precedent of #85, #86, #90.

## References

- Design spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-05-chunk-docs-github-coupling-design.md`
- `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-03-chunk-driven-implementation-design.md` §8 step 5
- `docs/AGENTIC_ORCHESTRATION_HANDBOOK.md` §11.2
- `scripts/agentic/run_log.py` (existing writer)
- Commit `58963df` (manual reconciliation that motivated this)

`chunks complete <name> --pr-url <url>` appends a row to `docs/AGENTIC_RUN_LOG.md` using `run_log.append_chunk_row`.
